sections.heading: Build with MongoDB on AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud
sections.body:
MongoDB Atlas makes it easy to deploy and manage databases
on-demand when and where you need them.

Deploy a Database

sections.heading: Step 1: Choose a cluster type.
sections.body:
Play around with a free cluster, launch a Flex cluster, or define a dedicated cluster
configuration for your application.

To choose a deployment type, see
[Clusters](ref-deployment-types).

sections.heading: Step 2: Choose a Cloud Provider and Region
sections.body:
Deploy your database to the same cloud provider and region as
your applications to reduce latency and standardize security
controls.

To choose a cloud provider and region, see [Cloud Providers and Regions](create-cluster-cloud-provider-region).

sections.heading: Step 3: Customize your cluster.
sections.body:
Enable multi-cloud and multi-region data distribution to
expand global coverage, increase fault tolerance, and meet
data compliance requirements.

To customize your cluster, see [Configure High Availability and Workload Isolation](create-cluster-multi-region).

![MongoDB Atlas Deploy a Database image](/images/atlas-plp/atlas-plp-deploy-db.svg)

Secure your Database

sections.heading: Step 1: Add IP Access List Entries
sections.body:
Define an IP access list for your cluster.
Always-on authentication ensures only approved client
connections can access your database.

To add IP Access List entries, see [IP Access List](access-list).

sections.heading: Step 2: Manage Database Users
sections.body:
Define how your team members and applications authenticate
to your database and what data they can access.

To manage database users, see [Configure Database Users](mongodb-users).

sections.heading: Step 3: (Optional) Configure Private Network Access
sections.body:
Configure peering connections or private endpoints for your
applications to connect to your databases for additional
security controls.

To configure peering connections, see [VPC/VNet peering](vpc-peering).
To configure private endpoints, see [private endpoint connections](private-endpoint).

![MongoDB Atlas Secure your Database image](/images/atlas-plp/atlas-plp-secure-db.svg)

Connect to your Database

sections.heading: Step 1: Choose a Connection Type
sections.body:
Connect to your database using the MongoDB Shell, one of
MongoDB's client libraries, MongoDB Compass, or the
MongoDB Connector for BI.

To connect to your database, see [connection string is valid](atlas-connect-to-deployment).

sections.heading: Step 2: Interact with your Data
sections.body:
Use your chosen connection type to view your data, import
documents, and run queries.

To insert data, see [Insert and View a Document](gswa-insert-data).

![MongoDB Atlas Connect to your Database image](/images/atlas-plp/atlas_plp.gif)

Optimize your Database

sections.heading: Step 1: Create Custom Alerts
sections.body:
Customize default alerts or create new ones to receive
notifications based on events and metrics that you define.

To create custom alerts, see [configure your alert settings](alerts).

sections.heading: Step 2: Review Index and Schema Suggestions
sections.body:
See on-demand index and schema suggestions for your
collections to improve your application's query performance.

To review suggestions, see [analyze slow queries](analyze-slow-queries) and
[improve your schema](schema-suggestions).

sections.heading: Step 3: Automate Archival of Cold Data
sections.body:
Define archival rules to move infrequently accessed data
into fully managed cloud object storage while retaining full
queryability of that data.

To archive your data, see [Online Archives](online-archive-overview).

![MongoDB Atlas Optimize your Database image](/images/atlas-plp/atlas-plp-optimize-db.svg)

Related Products & Resources

sections.heading: Go Further with Atlas
sections.body:
Explore other data services available with Atlas.

Build full-text search on top of your data.

Integrate Atlas into your workflows.
